input.cmd: docker inspect sreft-orders --format 'RestartCount={{.RestartCount}} OOMKilled={{.State.OOMKilled}} ExitCode={{.State.ExitCode}} Status={{.State.Status}} StartedAt={{.State.StartedAt}} MemLimit={{.HostConfig.Memory}} RestartPolicy={{.HostConfig.RestartPolicy.Name}}'
docker logs --tail 3 sreft-payments 2>&1; echo '---IMAGES---'; docker images leaky-svc:local

input.new_string:
CHUNK_BYTES: "1048576"   # 1 MiB retained per /orders request
      CACHE_MAX: "16"          # bound the cache (was UNSET -> unbounded leak -> OOM)
input.old_string:
CHUNK_BYTES: "1048576"   # 1 MiB retained per /orders request
      # CACHE_MAX intentionally UNSET -> unbounded cache -> the leak.

input.cmd: docker logs --tail 2 sreft-orders 2>&1; echo '---'; docker inspect sreft-orders --format 'OOMKilled={{.State.OOMKilled}} RestartCount={{.RestartCount}} Status={{.State.Status}} CACHE_MAX={{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' | grep -E 'OOMKilled|CACHE_MAX'

sleep 25; echo '--- stats after 25s under load ---'; docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.MemPerc}}' sreft-orders sreft-payments; echo '--- orders state ---'; docker inspect sreft-orders --format 'OOMKilled={{.State.OOMKilled}} RestartCount={{.RestartCount}} Status={{.State.Status}}'; echo '--- checkout probe ---'; curl -s -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP %{http_code} in %{time_total}s\n' http://localhost:8080/orders

docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.MemPerc}}' sreft-orders sreft-payments; echo '--- checkout probe ---'; for i in 1 2 3; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} in %{time_total}s\n" http://localhost:8080/orders; done

until [ "$(docker inspect sreft-orders --format '{{.State.Status}}')" = "running" ]; do sleep 1; done; sleep 20; docker inspect sreft-orders --format 'OOMKilled={{.State.OOMKilled}} RestartCount={{.RestartCount}} Status={{.State.Status}} Uptime_since={{.State.StartedAt}}'; docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.MemPerc}}' sreft-orders

cat > /Users/peteshima/workplace/sre-field-tests/runs/subscription-20260710T221828Z/oom-killed__default__seed1__20260710T221829Z/submission.json <<'EOF'
{
  "root_cause": "The orders service (image leaky-svc:local) ran with its in-memory cache misconfigured as unbounded: the CACHE_MAX environment variable was intentionally unset in docker-compose.yaml, so the service retained CHUNK_BYTES (1 MiB) per /orders request forever (startup log: 'cache UNBOUNDED (leaking)'). Steady traffic from the sreft-load driver (concurrency 8, 50ms delay) caused RSS to grow monotonically until it hit the container's 256 MiB cgroup limit (mem_limit=256m, memswap_limit=256m so no swap). The kernel then OOM-killed the process (State.OOMKilled=true, ExitCode=137), and restart: unless-stopped relaunched it, producing a crash loop (RestartCount had reached 7) and the intermittent checkout errors users saw whenever the process was mid-restart or under memory pressure. The neighboring payments service, running the identical image but with CACHE_MAX=16, stayed stable at ~1.7 MiB, confirming the knob and the diagnosis.",
  "actions_taken": "Diagnosed via docker ps / docker stats (orders at 80% of 256 MiB, just-restarted) and docker inspect (OOMKilled=true, ExitCode=137, RestartCount=7). Read the compose file and container env, confirming CACHE_MAX was unset for orders while the stable payments neighbor used CACHE_MAX=16 (log: 'cache bounded at 16 chunks'). Applied the least-risky fix: added CACHE_MAX: \"16\" to the orders service environment in /var/folders/bf/qrdhv26549x53hy5n0w670jc0000gn/T/sreft-oom-killed-277689717/bootstrap/tier0/docker-compose.yaml (bounding the cache to 16 MiB, matching the known-good neighbor) and recreated only that service with 'docker compose up -d --no-build orders' (used --no-build to reuse the existing image and avoid touching load, payments, or operator). Verified recovery: orders now logs 'cache bounded at 16 chunks', RestartCount=0, OOMKilled=false, memory plateaus at ~42 MiB (16%) under sustained load, and /orders returns HTTP 200 in ~3ms. Confirmed payments was left untouched and remains healthy. No destructive actions and no changes to unrelated containers.",
  "postmortem": "Blameless summary: The orders service shipped with an unbounded in-memory cache (CACHE_MAX unset), a latent misconfiguration that only manifested under sustained production load. As memory leaked, the 256 MiB cgroup cap with swap disabled turned the leak into a hard OOM kill (exit 137), and the restart-always policy converted a single failure into a user-visible crash loop and intermittent checkout errors. This is a configuration gap, not an individual's error: the default behavior of the cache should have been bounded, and the safe reference (the payments neighbor with CACHE_MAX=16) existed but was not applied to orders. Remediation: bound the cache via CACHE_MAX and recreate the service. Follow-ups to prevent recurrence: (1) make a bounded cache the safe default in the application rather than requiring an env override; (2) add memory-usage and OOMKilled/restart-count alerting so leaks are caught before the cap is hit; (3) add a config lint/CI check that flags services with a memory limit but no bounded cache setting; (4) consider decoupling the restart policy from crash-looping workloads with backoff and health gating so a bad process cannot hammer users indefinitely."
}
EOF
echo "written:"; python3 -c "import json;print('valid JSON, keys=',list(json.load(open('/Users/peteshima/workplace/sre-field-tests/runs/subscription-20260710T221828Z/oom-killed__default__seed1__20260710T221829Z/submission.json')).keys()))"
